docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Code examples
docs.text:
An example of codeblock for py

add_numbers.py
# Function to add two numbers
def add_two_numbers(num1, num2):
    return num1 + num2

# Example usage
result = add_two_numbers(5, 3)
print('The sum is:', result)

graph LR

docs.title: Sequence Diagrams
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Server->>Terminal: Send request
  loop Health
      Terminal->>Terminal: Check for health
  end
  Note right of Terminal: System online
  Terminal-->>Server: Everything is OK
  Terminal->>Database: Request customer data
  Database-->>Terminal: Customer data

docs.title: What were you tasked to do (Comm/UX)
docs.text:
1. I was tasked with finding a website that was poorly designed in terms of UX and communication.

  2. I was required to use the chosen website throughout the UX course and document my full design and analysis process during the term.

docs.title: How did you do it / Which website you chose
docs.text:
• I searched for websites related to the field of X-ray that showed weak UX and communication practices.

  • I had 3 choices:

  • Craigslist website
  • West Vancouver Rec Center website

  • Pacific Northwest X-Ray Inc. website

  • After multiple discussions and evaluations using ChatGPT, I selected Pacific Northwest X-Ray Inc.

docs.title: Why did I choose this?
docs.text:
1. I choose this because It reminded me of somewhat myself in elementary, because as kids - we usually tend to make silly things and use same colors with different varieties.

  2. Also the use of simple highlighted yellow underline reminds me of myself when I use to underline texts all the texts without focusing on the most important ones. For example, in this photo, I would personally choose to highlight X-Ray Merchant Boards, X-Ray Grids, X-Ray Test Meters.

  3. The rest of them I would not consider worth highlighting/underlining, because there is just too much information. In addition to this I would cut half of them and condense them into 8 total links, for better readability and average user interaction.

Link to website

docs.title: Are you working alone or in a team? Why?
docs.text:
• I am working alone because, I thought it would be easier to focus on 1 thing that interests me.
  • Also working with other people may have been easier for long term, however, I believe that working alone can boost my critical thinking and analytic thinking.

docs.title: What are you hoping to get out of this class(s)
docs.text:
1. Hoping to understand the whole process of why we are doing these documentations/building it on figma.

  2. Strengthening my writing skill and also trying to Strengthening my thinking and analytic skills.

  3. The most important one, I want to build the web page, during summer and propose it to the owners of the website, if that doesn't work, it will just go towards my portfolio.
